Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Malik Washington scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Malik Washington does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

Resolution source: https://www.nfl.com/scores

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fight between Ismail Naurdiev and Ryan Loder at UFC Fight Night: Tsarukyan vs. Hooker, scheduled for November 22, 2025, ends by KO or TKO, including referee stoppage, doctor stoppage, or corner stoppage. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

Resolution source: https://www.ufc.com/events

[mkt_690352_doc_008] includes UFC stats showing a KO/TKO result associated with Ryan Loder, reinforcing that at least one side has recent stoppage upside. Overall, the evidence is still indirect and stylistic rather than concrete event-on-track proof, so I keep the estimate only moderately above base rate rather than near 0.5 or higher.

Description: This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position in the Sprint Qualifying at the 2025 F1 Brazilian Grand Prix, scheduled for Nov 7, 2025.

If the 2025 F1 Brazilian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Nov 14, 2025, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the Sprint Qualifying session for the 2025 F1 Brazilian Grand Prix.
